claim_text: Across all nine Salt cases, left_lower_leg effective thermal metrics remain scrutiny-cleared and can support Salt-only branch dependency work on the left-side return path.
supporting_row_subset: salt_all / left_lower_leg / effective_htc (headline_eligible); salt_all / left_lower_leg / effective_ua (headline_eligible); salt_all / left_lower_leg / thermal_resistance (headline_eligible)
caveat: These remain effective, support-gated quantities rather than intrinsic closure coefficients.

claim_text: Across all nine Salt cases, test_section_span is the cleanest thermal anchor: all branch rows remain usable and stay comfortably above the current small-delta-T floor.
supporting_row_subset: salt_all / test_section_span / effective_htc (headline_eligible); salt_all / test_section_span / effective_ua (headline_eligible); salt_all / test_section_span / thermal_resistance (headline_eligible)
caveat: Interpret these as effective transport indicators, not unrestricted local HTC truth.

claim_text: Across all nine Salt cases, left_upper_leg remains Salt-only headline-eligible because usable fractions stay high and the minimum driving temperature stays well above the masking floor.
supporting_row_subset: salt_all / left_upper_leg / effective_htc (headline_eligible); salt_all / left_upper_leg / effective_ua (headline_eligible); salt_all / left_upper_leg / thermal_resistance (headline_eligible)
caveat: Still support-gated and branch-specific; do not reinterpret as wall-law closure evidence.

claim_text: The derived upcomer remains a defensible Salt-only branch surface because all three component spans stay scrutiny-cleared in the Salt family and the derived branch inherits high usable fractions.
supporting_row_subset: salt_all / upcomer / effective_htc (headline_eligible); salt_all / upcomer / effective_ua (headline_eligible); salt_all / upcomer / thermal_resistance (headline_eligible)
caveat: Corners and junctions remain omitted from the upcomer coordinate and must stay omitted in any model mapping.

claim_text: Salt right_leg thermal rows remain blocked from headline use even though the mean driving temperature is not small, because usable fraction stays near 0.73 and the failure mode is persistent area-ratio masking.
supporting_row_subset: salt_all / right_leg / effective_htc (internal_only); salt_all / right_leg / effective_ua (internal_only); salt_all / right_leg / thermal_resistance (internal_only)
caveat: Do not promote right_leg thermal behavior without a revised branch support path.

claim_text: Across all four Water cases, left_lower_leg effective thermal metrics are blocked by small |T_bulk - T_wall| and low usable fraction; this branch should not be used for Water-family HTC, UA', or R'_th fitting.
supporting_row_subset: water_all / left_lower_leg / effective_htc (excluded); water_all / left_lower_leg / effective_ua (excluded); water_all / left_lower_leg / thermal_resistance (excluded)
caveat: The large numerical mean HTC values are denominator-sensitive artifacts, not evidence of stronger local heat transfer.

claim_text: Water test_section_span is the cleanest Water thermal branch, but it remains supporting-only rather than headline-safe because the minimum resolved driving temperature stays below the current 0.50 K scrutiny threshold.
supporting_row_subset: water_all / test_section_span / effective_htc (contextual_only); water_all / test_section_span / effective_ua (contextual_only); water_all / test_section_span / thermal_resistance (contextual_only)
caveat: Useful for internal comparison, not yet for Water-family fitting claims.

claim_text: Across both Salt and Water, effective thermal metrics are not uniformly interpretable branch-by-branch; branch promotion gates are required because support quality and resolved driving temperature vary systematically by branch and family.
supporting_row_subset: cross_family / left_lower_leg / effective_htc (excluded); cross_family / test_section_span / effective_htc (supporting_only); cross_family / upcomer / effective_htc (excluded)
caveat: This is a methodological conclusion about interpretability, not a fitted cross-family thermal dependency.

claim_text: Boundary-layer landmarks remain contextual evidence only; they can help explain branch behavior but should not be used as headline model evidence or closure targets.
caveat: The current scrutiny package keeps all boundary-layer rows internal-only by design.

claim_text: Momentum resistance should remain a proxy diagnostic rather than a direct model target because it inherits the same branchwise disagreement and support limits as the underlying direct-versus-shear pressure reductions.
caveat: Use only as a derived consistency check until the direct hydraulic contradictions are resolved more fully.

claim_text: The main Salt-vs-Water difference is interpretability, not just magnitude: Salt left-side return-path branches are scrutiny-cleared, whereas Water left-side return-path branches remain support-limited by low |T_bulk - T_wall| and inherited masking.
supporting_row_subset: salt_all / left_lower_leg / effective_htc (headline_eligible); water_all / left_lower_leg / effective_htc (excluded); salt_all / upcomer / effective_htc (headline_eligible); water_all / upcomer / effective_htc (excluded)
caveat: Do not collapse this into a cross-family coefficient ranking.

claim_text: Water 1 left_lower_leg hydraulic disagreement is best interpreted as a weak-signal wall-registered p_rgh issue: the branch-end cumulative direct p_rgh drop stays positive, but the branch-mean direct gradient oscillates around zero because mixed-sign local derivatives nearly cancel.
caveat: Treat the contradiction as a resolved exclusion rather than a usable hydraulic dependency. The direct p_rgh signal is about one order of magnitude smaller than the shear-implied branch drop (terminal ratio 0.104), and the mean direct gradient is effectively a weak-signal average over mixed-sign local derivatives.

claim_text: Water 2 left_lower_leg hydraulic disagreement is now best treated as a resolved exclusion: branch-end cumulative direct p_rgh drop remains positive for every retained time, one retained window carries the only branch alignment-sign flip, and the remaining branch-mean direct sign changes are weak-signal cancellation artifacts rather than stable reversal.
caveat: Resolved exclusion. Water 2 left_lower_leg does not show evidence of a stable branchwise pressure-drop reversal. All retained times keep a positive branch-end cumulative direct p_rgh drop, one retained time carries a unique alignment-sign flip relative to the modal branch orientation, and the remaining sign changes in the branch-mean direct gradient are weak-signal finite-difference cancellations rather than robust reversals.

claim_text: Within the matched Salt 2 trio, upcomer and test_section_span remain the cleanest mechanism branches for thermal comparison; the right_leg should stay out of mechanism ranking even though its mean effective metrics are finite.
supporting_row_subset: salt2 / upcomer / effective_htc (headline_eligible); salt2 / test_section_span / effective_htc (headline_eligible); salt2 / right_leg / effective_htc (internal_only)
caveat: This is a Salt 2 mechanism result only, not a universal loop ranking.

claim_text: Water upper_leg and upcomer rows should remain internal-only because denominator collapse, not just masking, controls their apparent thermal ratios in multiple Water cases.
supporting_row_subset: water_all / upper_leg / effective_htc (excluded); water_all / upcomer / effective_htc (excluded)
caveat: Large numerical HTC or low R'_th values in these branches are not model-ready evidence without stronger bulk-temperature support.

claim_text: Cross-family hydraulic dependency construction on left_lower_leg remains blocked because both Water rows are exclusions from the evidence subset, even though Water 2 no longer requires an unresolved-sign label.
caveat: This is an exclusion boundary, not evidence of cross-family hydraulic similarity or difference.
